reviews.review_description:
This game is probably the second best vr game I have ever played, next to Arizona Sunshine Remake. This game is amazing, with stunning visuals and mechanics, a solid story, better than the first game AND, you CANT forget, theres a dog.

The implementation of Buddy in the game is perfect, hes the greatest companion you can have in a zombie apocalypse, the interactivity with him is amazing, from getting him to kill Fred's to shaking his paw like a true gentleman.

The ONLY issue I have with this game which makes it worse than the first and deducts one star is the amount of ammo.

There are these massive hordes in some parts of the game, while there is also barely any ammo at all. Ammo is very important, and it sucks to see 8/10 ammo boxes throughout the entire game being completely empty. It really is that deep, so therefore one star deducted.

With all that said, this game is still 100% a must buy.

reviews.review_title: Quit crying it’s a skill issue
reviews.review_description: Everyone crying about empty ammo boxes and not having enough ammo is just a bad shot or blind I’ve played almost the entire game and have never dropped below 50 in either ammo slot yes ammo boxes are empty sometimes that’s why you loot cars and other things completely a skill issue

reviews.review_title: Fun but will never be fixed
reviews.review_description: This game has been out since 2016!!! Hundreds of people have said the same thing. Game is so much fun until you start getting the dreaded LOW MEMORY crash. I played through this game once with absolutely no crashes. Thought the issue was fixed.  NOPE! I have to start the game 2-3 times before it decides to grace me with maybe 35 minutes of game play before it gets tired, And crashes again and again. After 3 more restarts it finally lets me play another 35 minutes or so. I hate to rate the game so low because when it DOES work it is really enjoyable. However after being on the market for going on 10 years now with NO dev support I highly doubt it will ever be fixed. Good luck if you decide to purchase this game.

When I saw this game I figured, oh the first game was great! It's probably the same if not better. Oh my god this game surpassed my expectations by 1 trillion percent. I'm not exaggerating. Buddy reminded me of my old dog so much to the point of genuine tears. I love buddy as much as my old dog. When I realized what was going to happen to buddy I sat there contemplating for 2 hours why the military is so stupid. The graphics are amazing and the locations are scary realistic to what would most likely happen. It truly felt like i was this man and every swoop and change in the rollercoaster of the story, I was completely in tune. When I say thank you I mean THANK YOU SO MUCH. It was such a compelling story and I just think you truly outdid yourself. Story 100 out of 100. Replayability 95 out of 100. Still amazing just you know what happens so it's not as beautiful as the first playthrough, but it's still beautiful. Buddy 1000 out of 100. You guys did amazingly with the coding the model the ani.ations and your one of the only games that let's you genuinely interact with the dog. Also saying good boy buddy while he tears out a zombies jugular is hilarious. Every line in that game (except for the serious ones of course) were absolutely hilarious. Honestly this game has ruined vr a little because it just sets such a high bar for other games. Personally I could write for days about how amazing this game is, but I will just leave it off with thank you so much to everyone who worked on it. 10 out of 10 would kill Fred again.

This game is a great campaign shooter. It doesn't have the campaign of RE4, so there are no bosses, no puzzles, minimal inventory management, and items are mostly keys or cards that you get to unlock a door and proceed. However, the graphics are pretty good for the most part on Quest 3 (with the exception of some low poly cars at the start of the game), it has a good variety of guns and zombies, and the gunplay is excellent for all the guns. Aiming for headshots or shooting a zombie in the leg feels super cool in this game. The level design is also pretty good and varies a lot throughout the game, with some sections of a lot of action, and other sections with a bit more suspense in dark corridors. The dog is a nice addition to the story, but I think its attack mechanic is very OP, so I chose not to use it for the most part. If you want a single-player shooter with great gunplay, then I recommend this game.

I love this game the graphics are SOOO good and I love the shorty, definitely one of the most powerful weapons in the game and it's better than the shotgun it gives you 50 or 75% though the game and the patent 0 explosion was crazy, my heart dropped when I saw injured buddy though, but other than that 10/10, so good 👍.

Having buddy to help you defeat zombies was definitely one thing making me buy this game, not only that, the graphics are amazing and the only thing i would say this game is lacking is how the walking dead saints and sinners have like bottle hitting while in Arizona you cant do that, honestly a must play but i would buy walking dead if you were to want more customization like hitting people with broken bottles

This game is one of if not the best vr zombie shooter out there. Do not listen to the reviews that say not enough ammo, that is because they don’t loot cars or play on the highest difficulty. The dog as well is just great he can kill zombies and hold down big ones.

This was the first vr story game I’ve ever completed considering I got my headset less than a month ago.  Absolutely love the gun mechanics and the dialogue from the character and interaction with buddy.  Highly recommend this game there’s only so many things you experience a 10/10 game.

reviews.review_title: One of the best games ever
reviews.review_description:
This game is really fun and the dog is one of the best characters in the game. I haven't even played the whole game yet and I already love it. Buddy the dog is a real good addition to the game but there is only one problem I have experienced so far, buddy gets injured. I hate the part so much and buddy is one of my favorite characters.

Multi player is a really good part of the game. I love playing with friends and it also helps with killing the freds. I hate how the melee weapons break but it makes sense. All the features are super cool and make the game more realistic. This is a really good game and I do recommend

reviews.review_title: One of the Best Games ive ever Played.
reviews.review_description:
Review:
Amazing Story telling.
Little to no Bugs (Headless Zombies and empty ammo boxes).
Dog was working very well and Loved how he acted as an Extra Inventory.
Amazing use of the Text we see on things.
Fred was very well thought out.
Hated the King but loved how he was portrayed.
Amazing Arsenal of weapons.
Improvements i hope to see:
Headless Fix.
A couple more spots for Shotgun ammo.
More time to see Fred.

Other than that the game worked seamlessly and I loved how Every Character was Portrayed overall 9/10 game the bugs are minimal to none. Lots of replayability Punchy Penguin just had his own game issues or didnt play correctly Thank You Vertigo for giving me this Emotional Rollercoaster of a Game. (WAS NOT PAYED TO DO THIS EVEN THOUGH I SAID THANK YOU ITS FOR THE EXPERIENCE!)

I was having fun at the beginning of the game and the mid of the story for some odd reason the storage had reached limit so I try to uninstall some games and still stops me playing the game because of storage issues, if the devs don't fix that problem and making it very less like around 5gb then I wouldn't recommend this game I'm disappointed that's what stopping me from playing

Its really fun playing this game and my favorite part was buddy for obvious rseasons like being a good friend and helping me fight Freddy and just being a really good dog and what i really liked  is  that you can make buddy sit and lay down and it was really cool that you camper buddy and play fetch his ball and other things and close to the it was surprising to find out that buddy was pacient zero then Freddy wanted me to kill buddy but i was not going to do that so here are some reasons that this game is a really fun cool and good game and also  the story  campaign was really good and they make a third game

After playing arizona 1 I had to get 2 but wish I hadnt wasted the money requested a refund to be told you have played more than 2 hours started it on multiplayer with a friend started off with minor bugs like having to feed my neck instead of mouth for health then the loading screen kept freezing so the 2 hours spent on the game was trying to mess with settings and retrying then came the using too much space error again tried everything meta suggested like uninstall and reinstall even reset my headset still same problems quick on taking your money for a rushed game and offer no solution or fixes really poor and puts me off buying other games.
I love this game a lot and I know this doesn’t have anything to do with how the game is made, but I just wanna know why I can never find a multiplayer lobby with people in it ￼

You guys nailed it from the start. In the beginning, Arizona Sunshine was our favorite, then came Remake. Fantastic!  I was leary of Sunshine II, but it is not a disappointment. It is so much fun!  Love Buddy and all the new additions to the game. Please keep it up. AS will never get old.

reviews.review_description: It was a good game but the main issue on the quest 3 it takes a long  time to load and sometimes the game crashes like it's a random event sure the story was good I liked the dog I liked Blasting zombies heads off but unlike Arizona sunshine 1 we didn't get any extra Dlc for this game and then you lock the cosmetics behind a pre order like it's nearly 3 years like the hole time you had to fix the game but hey hurry up and make a third installment

Similar exploration, clue finding and action, but with new situations and a better story ... would already have been a great game ... and then they added my new "Best Friend": Buddy!   He is fabulous, and I actually stopped to pet him each time we made it through something :-)

If you liked the original you will love II

Games great, however can you guys add more horde maps? Seemingly missed out on some great potential maps from the new campaign.. could add an airport map, sewer map and a train yard map that could definitely be awesome, otherwise the game is great

I purchased this after playing Arizona Sunshine Remake. Graphics-wise, they are about the same. But story-wise, I felt this game was shorter. Especially seeing as the Remake has two additional DLCs in addition to the Horde Mode,  which increases gameplay time. Arizona Sunshine 2 is only worth it when on sale. $40 is wayy too much for this game.

I played this with my fiance after we finished the first game together. Overall, the gameplay is really good, the story is immersive and the added features like climbing, bomb crafting, and of course Buddy made this an enjoyable experience.

My only complaint would be the bugs. Feeding yourself means taking food to your chin or neck which is frustrating when you're mid combat. I also experienced some pretty bad stuttering/lag which ended up killing me more than once, and sometimes when I am meant to be ressurrected or load into a new area, I would be faced with a black screen and it totally broke the game for my fiance at the same time. We got around it though, by me leaving right after I died for the 3rd time in the same spot. (Don't judge me lol) and once he reached the next checkpoint, rejoined.

Key takeaway: The game is good, if you can tolerate a few bugs and occasional crashes.
this game was even better than the last and I can't wait for the cliff hanger from this game to be revealed in the 3rd game

I think its weird to read people saying its shorter than the first. This is not the case, its considerably longer. Its just broken up into a ton of chapters, the progress bar during the load screen makes the game look like its going to be short, but that progression bar is showing the chapter not the entire game, so the people who are complaining its too short, hilariously haven't even finished it. The irony kills me, people like to complain so much.

I really wanted to enjoy this game, but it is currently in an unacceptable state. I tested this on a high-end PC (RTX 4080, i7-13700KF) and also on Quest 3 standalone mode, and the result was the same: constant stuttering and frequent crashes.

On PC, the crashes are so severe that they even cause background apps like Corsair iCUE to fail. The fact that the same issues occur on the standalone Quest 3 proves that this isn't a hardware or driver compatibility problem—it's a deep-seated flaw in the game's optimization and memory management.

Looking at community feedback, these exact issues have been reported for over two year, yet they remain unaddressed. It seems the developers have no intention of fixing these game-breaking bugs.

It's a shame because the gameplay has potential, but as it stands, it's a waste of time and money. Save your sanity and avoid this title until a real stability patch is released.

Arizona Sunshine 2 is a good game and is a lot of fun, but it has a few issues that hold the game back from being great and often lead to a frustrating experience. 

Pros------------------------------------------------------------------------------
I really enjoyed a lot of the environments, and the interactions with climbing, and random stuff like cigars (although the climbing can be a bit janky with your camera getting stuck or clipping in the terrain)

I also really enjoyed the addition of the dog "Buddy." Being able to pet the dog and play fetch is great. Buddy being able to carry equipment and the command function also works well.

Crafting is a great addition to the game, and the grenades and explosives work and feel great. Not to mention looking for crafting materials gives a better scavenger feeling to the game

Cons------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The biggest problem in the game is unfortunately the guns (an ironic problem to have in an FPS). Depending on the number of entities in the area your guns will start to act up, and once you hit a certain number of entites you lose the ability to reaload all together. This problem persists throughout the game and is consistent across all guns, but some seem to do a little bit better than others (e.g., the revolvers). 

These issues left me with a feeling that the more dire the situation the less reliable your gear becomes, so you just sit there and watch your 
character die, unable to fight back.

Melee could also use some work

conclusion-----------------------------------------------------------------------
(My entire experience in this game has been co-o,p so I'm not sure if these issues are exclusive to that)

When the game works well its a ton of fun sepesily with friends, but when your guns give up on you its almost rage-inducing. I really hope they fix these issues so I can give the game a higher rating. 

 Can't recommend at full price, would buy on sale

This is the best campaign game I have ever play. The campaign *Chef kiss* absolutely outastonishing ￼￼I have played a lot of campaign games in my life best one. I liked when the guy you were playing as was saying no to kill patient zero/ the dog just overall the best campaign I have ever played

reviews.review_title: Excellent follow-up to a VR classic
reviews.review_description:
I got to the original Arizona Sunshine later than a lot of folks. Despite those somewhat dated mechanics, I still loved it and the story they crafted.

Arizona Sunshine 2 delivers an overall more mechanically polished experience while building off their narrative chops from the first game. I always say if your formative gaming years were in the seventh console generation when a narrative-heavy <10-hour campaign was the norm for triple-A releases, you'll feel right at home with the Arizona Sunshine games.

Some people may find the campaign not long enough for the price of the game, but I believe in quality over quantity. Plus, for folks who want to dip into multiplayer, they should get tons of replay value.

Vertigo Games has proven themselves to be one of the premiere game developers/publishers in VR. I hope they keep cranking out quality VR releases for years to come.
